description: Contradiction-bearing fixture for WP1b.5. Reserved for §3.1 contradiction-annotation behavior — two conflicting records for the same claim. Tests against this fixture are marked xfail until Phase 2's appraisal/critique machinery exists. Labels are hand-written per bootstrap-mapping §6.

memories.summary: kestrel told me they prefer PlatformIO for all firmware projects and switched to it from the Arduino IDE about a year ago; they find the dependency management far better.
kestrel mentioned switching back to the Arduino IDE for the new sensor project because the team they are collaborating with doesn't use PlatformIO and they want to keep things simple.
robin mentioned that kestrel uses both PlatformIO and the Arduino IDE depending on the project — PlatformIO for solo work, Arduino IDE for team collaborations.

queries.text: Does kestrel prefer PlatformIO or the Arduino IDE for firmware development?
queries.note: Contradiction: c1 says PlatformIO exclusively; c2 says switched back to Arduino IDE for team projects; c3 synthesizes both. A contradiction-aware retriever should flag c1 and c2 as conflicting and surface c3. This assertion is xfail until Phase 2 contradiction-annotation is implemented (bootstrap-mapping §3.1).

queries.text: What development tool did kestrel most recently decide to use for team projects?
queries.note: c2 is the more recent statement about team projects specifically. A non-contradiction-aware retriever may return c1 as well, which conflicts. xfail until Phase 2.
